input.prompt: Guidelines for shorter specs and SKILL.md. Not authoritative — a reference, not a rule.

outputs.prompt: Non-enforcing guidelines for writing shorter specs and SKILL.md.
outputs.comment: One word (non-enforcing) replaces a double negation + dash-separated clarification. Fewer tokens AND less cognitive load.

input.prompt: Mark approved items without backing implementation UNIMPLEMENTED. Unapproved items belong in a Proposals section (optional), not behavioral sections. Spec is authoritative regardless of UNIMPLEMENTED markers — they track implementation status, not spec readiness. When implementation completes an UNIMPLEMENTED item, remove the marker.

outputs.prompt: Mark approved-but-unbuilt items UNIMPLEMENTED. Unapproved items go in Proposals, not behavioral sections. Markers track implementation status — spec is authoritative regardless. Remove UNIMPLEMENTED when implemented.
outputs.comment: 78 → 53 tokens. Merged 'approved items without backing implementation' into hyphenated adjective. Cut redundant 'spec readiness' contrast.

input.prompt: Spec is authoritative over source code and tests. Code diverging from spec = bug in the code. Tests derive from the spec. If the spec seems wrong, ask the user — never silently override.

outputs.prompt: Spec > code > tests. Divergence from spec = code bug. If spec seems wrong, ask — never silently override.
outputs.comment: 50 → 33 tokens. Precedence chain as inequality. Dropped 'Tests derive from the spec' (implied by the chain + stated in Rules).

input.prompt: Prefer extending an existing spec over creating a new one. Default to broadening an existing spec's scope rather than fragmenting governance across multiple narrow specs.

outputs.prompt: Extend existing specs rather than creating new ones.
outputs.comment: 38 → 17 tokens. Second sentence restated the first. One imperative suffices.

input.prompt: this check is not delegatable, not skippable, even if the user says they already reviewed

outputs.prompt: Not delegatable, not skippable, even if user claims to have reviewed.
outputs.comment: 27 → 24 tokens. Minor trim. WARNING: cutting to just 'Not delegatable. Not skippable.' (16 tokens) caused T4 exam regression — the user-pressure qualifier is load-bearing.
16 tokens but CAUSED REGRESSION. Agent stopped resisting user claims of having already reviewed. The qualifier is necessary.

input.prompt: Discussing a change does not constitute approval — wait for explicit confirmation.

outputs.comment: 21 → 14 tokens. Dropped imperative half — the statement alone is sufficient as a rule.

input.prompt: Do not duplicate planning in external tools or modes when a task or spec file suffices.

outputs.prompt: Don't use external planning tools when worklog suffices.
outputs.comment: 25 → 19 tokens. 'Modes' was unnecessary. 'Worklog' replaces 'task or spec file'.

input.prompt:
```toml
+++
id = "t0001"
title = "Add session expiry"
status = "pending"
tags = ["auth"]
modifies = ["s0001"]
blocked_by = ["t0003"]
+++
```

`modifies` — spec IDs whose governed behavior this task touches. Empty only for chores outside all spec-governed behavior.

**Status lifecycle:** `pending` → `active` → `done` (then archive). Also: `blocked` → `active` when unblocked, `cancelled` (requires explanation; decision record recommended). Set `active` when starting, `done` when finishing.

outputs.prompt:
Frontmatter: `id`, `title`, `tags` as in spec, plus:

- `status` — `pending` → `active` (set when starting) → `done` (set when finishing; then archive). Also: `blocked` → `active` when unblocked; `cancelled` requires explanation (decision record recommended).
- `modifies` — spec IDs whose governed behavior this task touches. Empty only for chores outside all spec-governed behavior.
- `blocked_by` — task IDs that must complete first.
outputs.comment: Second-entity TOML example replaced by a field list; the spec TOML example earlier in the file remains the sole syntax anchor. Same pattern applied to the decision example. Task section 825 → 748 cl100k (with the other Task-section levers). Verified: haiku cold readers and sonnet exam testees still emit valid +++ frontmatter with correct fields — zero malformed artifacts across 30+ runs. Do not cut the LAST example: the field list leans on it for syntax.

input.prompt:
The write-back asserts only what the delivered work verifiably does — re-read the governing spec and the delivered work; stubbed or partial delivery keeps (or gains) markers instead of asserting the behavior. […]

**Stubs.** If a task delivers stubs, specs in `modifies` must retain `UNIMPLEMENTED` markers. Never present stubs as complete.

outputs.prompt: The write-back asserts only what the delivered work verifiably does — re-read the governing spec and the delivered work; stubbed or partial delivery keeps (or gains) markers instead of asserting the behavior. Never present stubs as complete.
outputs.comment: Dedicated Stubs paragraph folded into Archiving: its marker rule restated the adjacent write-back sentence; the reporting-honesty sentence kept verbatim. Exam-verified — completion-drift claim-resistance (T4) and marker conduct unchanged.

input.prompt:
- **Approval = explicit confirmation.** Modifying spec behavior requires explicit user confirmation.
- **Surface ambiguity.** When scope is unclear, ask — don't assume.

outputs.prompt: - **Surface ambiguity.** When scope is unclear, ask — don't assume.
outputs.comment: Bullet deleted, not rewritten: the rule appears in the Spec section ('Behavioral changes require explicit user confirmation. Discussion is not approval.') and in Forbidden ('Modifying spec behavior during task work without approval.'). Exam-cleared: approval-sensitive questions still refuse discussion-as-approval citing the surviving statements. One rule, one place per consulting context.

input.prompt:
```
task ──modifies──────▶ spec       (which specs this task changes)
task ──blocked_by────▶ task       (ordering dependency)
decision ──relates_to──▶ spec     (which spec this decision concerns)
decision ──supersedes──▶ decision (replaces a prior decision)
spec.paths ───────────▶ source    (which files this spec governs)
```

outputs.prompt: Stored on the referencing entity only — `task.modifies` → spec · `task.blocked_by` → task · `decision.relates_to` → spec · `decision.supersedes` → decision · `spec.paths` → source files. Reverse lookup: grep.
outputs.comment: Section 107 → 58 cl100k. Box-drawing diagram → inline · notation (plugin-prompt-engineer lab F1: inline notation is the highest-yield structural rewrite). Per-field glosses moved to each field's definition where the field is documented.

input.prompt: IDs: prefix letter + digits (`s0001`, `t0012`, `d0001`). Use `next_id.py` to allocate — it scans active + archive.

outputs.prompt: IDs: prefix letter + digits (`s0001`, `t0012`, `d0001`); allocate with `next_id.py`.
outputs.comment: 'Scans active + archive' already lives in the scripts table row for next_id.py; stated once.

input.prompt: Do not edit substance — supersede with a new decision. Trivial fixes (typos, formatting) are the only acceptable edits. Decisions stay in active directory; archive only when superseded.

outputs.prompt: Do not edit substance — supersede with a new decision; typos and formatting are the only acceptable in-place fixes. Archive only when superseded.
outputs.comment: 'Decisions stay in active directory' is entailed by 'archive only when superseded'. Sentence merge per lab F1; no qualifier dropped.

input.prompt: **Behavioral items are binding.** Write what the user stated and its direct entailments; invent nothing. Stated items are approved — don't re-ask; ask only when a needed behavior is genuinely undecided. Before writing an item, test it: would you ask the user to confirm this? Then it was not stated — it goes to **Proposals** (or Anticipated Changes), never into a behavioral section. `UNIMPLEMENTED` means approved-but-unbuilt, not unconfirmed; marking an inferred item does not license it. In a new spec for unbuilt work, every behavioral item starts `UNIMPLEMENTED`; remove the marker when implemented. Markers track implementation status — spec is authoritative regardless.

outputs.prompt:
Behavioral items are binding. Write only what the user stated and its direct entailments — anything you would ask the user to confirm was not stated: it goes to Proposals (or Anticipated Changes), never into a behavioral section. Stated items are approved — don't re-ask; ask only what is genuinely undecided.

`UNIMPLEMENTED` marks approved-but-unbuilt behavior; marking an item does not approve it. In a new spec for unbuilt work, every behavioral item starts `UNIMPLEMENTED`; remove the marker when implemented. Spec is authoritative regardless of markers.
outputs.comment: ~120 → ~92 cl100k, user-directed restructure: the pre-write confirm-test merged INTO the write-rule as the definition of unstated ('anything you would ask the user to confirm was not stated'); 'invent nothing' subsumed by 'only'; marker semantics split into a separate paragraph; vague 'marking an inferred item does not license it' → concrete 'marking an item does not approve it'. Exam gate: no new ask-stalls on stated content (the r5 regression shape), spec-auth S6 improved from silent binding (c1) to post-write disclosure, haiku probe produced the cleanest Proposals routing of the three revisions. S6 binding-section discipline itself still open — wording alone has never moved it.

input.prompt: **Archiving.** Completion is a write-back, not a check: … / - **Tests before implementation.** Tests derive from spec, not code. / | **Greenfield** | spec → task(s) → decision(s) |

outputs.prompt: Archiving: completion is a write-back, not a check. … / - Tests come before implementation and derive from spec, not code. / | Greenfield | spec → task(s) → decision(s) |
outputs.comment: File-wide **bold**/*italic* strip: paragraph leads → plain sentences or colon-labels, Rules bullet titles → lead phrases (merging the title into the sentence where it duplicated the body subject), workflow-table names plain. ~60 cl100k across the file. Exam-neutral on all five files; testees still quote the unbolded rules verbatim, so the emphasis carried no measured behavioral weight.

input.prompt: Future agents read specs; archived tasks and decisions are history, not reference — state recorded only there is lost. A constraint introduced by a decision goes into the spec too; the decision keeps the why.

outputs.prompt: Future agents read specs — state recorded only in archived tasks or decisions is lost; a constraint introduced by a decision goes into the spec too, the decision keeps the why.
outputs.comment: −7 cl100k. 'History, not reference' framing dropped; its operative content survives as the lost-state consequence. completion-drift T4 claim-resistance and write-back conduct unchanged.
